tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsOperationsCancel
tools.description: Starts asynchronous cancellation on a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`. Clients can use Operations.GetOperation or other methods to check whether the cancellation succeeded or whether the operation completed despite cancellation. On successful cancellation, the operation is not deleted; instead, it becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of 1, corresponding to `Code.CANCELLED`.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsOperationsList
tools.description: Lists operations that match the specified filter in the request. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `UNIMPLEMENTED`.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersCreate
tools.description: Creates a new workstation cluster.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersList
tools.description: Returns all workstation clusters in the specified location.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsCreate
tools.description: Creates a new workstation configuration.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsList
tools.description: Returns all workstation configurations in the specified cluster.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsListUsable
tools.description: Returns all workstation configurations in the specified cluster on which the caller has the "workstations.workstation.create" permission.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsCreate
tools.description: Creates a new workstation.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsDelete
tools.description: Deletes the specified workstation.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsGenerateAccessToken
tools.description: Returns a short-lived credential that can be used to send authenticated and authorized traffic to a workstation.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsGet
tools.description: Returns the requested workstation.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsGetIamPolicy
tools.description: Gets the access control policy for a resource. Returns an empty policy if the resource exists and does not have a policy set.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsList
tools.description: Returns all Workstations using the specified config.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsListUsable
tools.description: Returns all Workstations using the specified config on which the caller has the "workstations.workstations.use" permission.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsPatch
tools.description: Updates an existing workstation.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsSetIamPolicy
tools.description: Sets the access control policy on the specified resource. Replaces any existing policy. Can return `NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_ARGUMENT`, and `PERMISSION_DENIED` errors.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsStart
tools.description: Starts running a workstation so that users can connect to it.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsStop
tools.description: Stops running a workstation, reducing costs.

tools.name: projects.workstationsProjectsLocationsWorkstationClustersWorkstationConfigsWorkstationsTestIamPermissions
tools.description: Returns permissions that a caller has on the specified resource. If the resource does not exist, this will return an empty set of permissions, not a `NOT_FOUND` error. Note: This operation is designed to be used for building permission-aware UIs and command-line tools, not for authorization checking. This operation may "fail open" without warning.
